style_description: Vintage comic book art style from the 1940s–1960s, rendered with bold black ink outlines, halftone dot patterns, and slightly off-register color printing that gives an authentic aged-press aesthetic. The color palette relies on saturated primary and secondary tones — deep reds, cobalt blues, golden yellows, and forest greens — printed flat with minimal gradients, occasionally showing deliberate ink bleed and paper yellowing at the edges. Lighting is dramatic and stylized, with hard cel-shaded shadows and strong rim lighting suggesting theatrical tension, reminiscent of classic pulp adventure or superhero panels. Texture evokes worn newsprint — subtle grain, faded ink, and visible Ben-Day dot screens layered over mid-tones — giving the entire image a tactile, hand-printed quality. Composition follows classic comic panel conventions: dynamic diagonal framing, expressive exaggerated poses, and a visual energy that feels simultaneously nostalgic and kinetic.
